scenario: Validate plugin session registration, bridge online state, command dispatch and visible Figma writeback.
steps: Open Figma Desktop and launch the AutoDesign plugin in the target file.
Confirm npm run plugin:status reports an online session with the expected file and page.
Generate the acceptance report and preflight artifacts with the active session id.
Run one read-only inspect command against frame 8:30 and save the preview artifact.
Send a targeted mutating command that sets the frame stroke to #00FF88 at 2px, then verify the visible result in Figma.
Attempt a cleanup via undo.undo-last, observe the partial rollback, then clear the remaining stroke with strokes.clear-stroke.
observations: plugin_session_ha630yzf stayed online during status -> acceptance:prep -> inspect -> send -> undo -> clear-stroke.
The targeted frame iPhone 17 - 1 (8:30) showed the expected visible green stroke after the writeback command.
The acceptance-only stroke was fully removed after the cleanup command, and the user confirmed the green outline disappeared from the canvas.

Finding: undo.undo-last only reverted strokes.set-weight from the two-capability batch and left strokes.set-stroke in place, so a targeted strokes.clear-stroke command was required to fully restore the frame.
follow_up: Investigate undo.undo-last so a multi-capability batch can roll back atomically instead of only reverting the last capability result.
